rag_rules: Use AGREE syntax: assume/guarantee for logical expressions, eq for stateful equations, const for constants, and assign for output bindings
Structure stateful equations with initialized temporal recurrence and nested if-then-else for branching logic
Place component type contracts in the type scope, implementation contracts in the implementation scope
Use logical operators (and, or, not, =>) and implication for clear contract expressions
generic_rules: Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for input assumptions and output guarantees
Relate visible outputs to visible inputs in dependency guarantees, avoiding implementation-only paths
Align helper declaration types with expression types (bool for comparisons, int/real for arithmetic)
Avoid AADL property names in AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports/constants
Use valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in const and eq declarations
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence
Branching logic should use nested if-then-else
Use AGREE logical operators for clear contract expressions
Align helper declaration types with expression types
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports/constants
Do not use invalid AGREE scalar type names (use int, real, bool instead)
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
AGREE scalar type names must be int, real, or bool
